text.value: ADVISE FOR CONCERN: You have tried your best to comfort your child. In addition, I want to add that it is very important to look into the child's eyes, hold them gently, and speak to them all the time. _Help the women to demonstrate again using the new techniques._

ADVISE FOR CONCERN: You have tried your best to distract the child from an unwanted behavior. In addition, I want to add that it is always good to draw/shift your child's attention to something positive. This is better for child development than scolding or hitting them. If a child does a good behavior, praise them so that they continue to do the good behavior. _Help the women to demonstrate again using the new techniques._
ADVISE FOR CONCERN</pAsk the caregiver to copy the child's movements, to follow the child's lead.

I want to discuss with you ways to correct the child when they are acting/doing an unwanted behavior. For example, maybe the child is trying to take a very hot cup of chai. This is bad, because the child might spill the cup and get burnt. Can you show me how you would correct the child in this situation?
Have you observed the caregiver comforting the child?
Have you observed the caregiver correcting the child?
Is not able to comfort child, and child does not look to caregiver for comfort.
Looks into child's eyes and talks softly to child, gently touches child or holds child closely.
How did the caregiver comfort the child and show love?
Distracts child from unwanted actions with appropriate toy or activity.
Scolds child or responds harshly to child.
How did the caregiver correct the child?
Does not move with child, or controls child's movements or is not responsive to child.
Moves toward and with child, and talks to or makes sounds with child, responds to child.
_During the visit, how does the caregiver interact and respond to the child's movements, activities or sounds?_
_For the following questions, observe interaction between caregiver and child if possible. If have not observed so far during visit, ask caregiver to demonstrate._

PRAISE: I can see that you do a very good job comforting the child and showing them love. You are very good at this. For example, it is very important to look into the child's eyes, hold them gently, and speak to them all the time.

PRAISE: I can see that you do a very good job distracting your child from unwanted behaviors. It is always good to draw/shift your child's attention to something positive. This is better for child development than scolding or hitting them. If a child does a good behavior, praise them so that they continue to do the good behavior.

I would like to talk about how you comfort the child and show them love. Let's pretend that your child is disturbed right now. Can you show me how you will act/behave toward the child in order to make them comfortable, and show them love?
What age matches/compares with the development of the child?

The child's age is months. Normal behaviors for children are:
->3 to 6 months
->Responds to sound ->Looks and and follows object with eyes ->Interested in faces ->Starts to reach for objects
->6 months up to 9 months:
->Smiles back when you smile at child ->Alerts caregiver when hungry ->Coos, makes noises to communicate ->Sits up on own ->Recognizes and responds to caregivers and colors
->9 months up to 12 months:
->Begins to babble and form first words ->Responds to caregivers verbally and physically ->Crawls ->Stands on own ->Walking on own or with assistance
->12 months up to 2 years:
->Walking on own ->Talks, by 2 years old begins to make very short sentences ->Begins to form questions ->Eats on own ->Sleep throughout the night
->2 years or older:
->Expresses desires, needs, questions, etc. forming full thoughts in sentence form and curious about world around them.

ASK: Are there any other family members or caregivers who could join us for a discussion about your child's development? In particular, encourage father to join if available. Welcome them to join the discussion. Also ask the mother to have her child join or be nearby.

ADVISE CONCERNCommunicating well with your baby/child is important to support a strong connection and healthy development. By talking to our children and telling them the names of things they recognise words and this helps them learn to talk.

ADVISE/CONCERN Playing is important for children. A child who has no opportunities to play will not develop healthily. Children learn by seeing what others do, copying and practicing new things. Play is a great way to help children learn and practice new skills! For example, when they shake a rattle they learn what sound it makes, they learn to move the rattle.
Does not talk to child or talks harshly to child
Looks into child's eyes and talks softly to child, asks questions
Tries to force smile or is not responsive to child
Can you show me how you talk to your child? Can you show me some of the things you to do when you want to make them smile? Indicate how caregiver talks to child:

ASK: How do you play with your child? Could you please show me the normal ways you play with your child? Indicate whether caregiver plays with child, e.g. with toy objects or word games, or other, appropriate for age.

PRAISEThat's great, communicating like this with your child supports healthy development. By talking to our children and telling them the names of things they recognise words and this helps them learn to talk.

PRAISE the way caregiver demonstrated playing with the child for their healthy development. Advise: Children learn by seeing what others do, copying and practicing new things. Play is a great way to help children learn and practice new skills! The more opportunities we give our children to play, the more information is given to their brain. For example, when they shake a rattle they learn what sound it makes, they learn to move the rattle. It also helps them physically. Let the child show you how they want to play, rather than forcing them to play a certain way.
Respond to your child's sounds and interests. Call the child's name, and see your child respond.
Good ways to communicate with your child at this age are to:
Ask your child simple questions. Respond to your child's attempts to talk. Show and talk about nature, pictures, and things.

Encourage your child to talk and answer your child's questions. Teach your child stories, songs and games. Talk about pictures or books. Children will learn words if you draw something then repeat the word. _Advise the caregiver that they can draw their own child-friendly picture books, they don't need to buy in the shop. Example toy: book with pictures, material to draw on, ball, swing, bicycle_
Tell your child the names of things and people. Show your child how to say things with hands, like 'bye bye.' Sample toy: doll.
Playing is important to support healthy development. At this age, good ways to play with your child are to:
Help your child count, name and compare things. Make simple toys for your child. Sample toys: Objects of different colors and shapes to sort, sticks.
Give your child clean, safe household things to handle, bang, and drop. Sample toys: containers with lids, metal pot and spoon.
Hide a child's favorite toy under a cloth or box. See if the child can find it. Play peek-a-boo.
Give your child things to stack up, and to put into containers and take out. Sample toys: Nesting and stacking objects, container and clothes clips.

Good ways to communicate with your child at this age are: (_demonstrate_)
->Smile and laugh with your child. Talk to your child. Get a conversation going by copying your child's sounds or gestures. _PRACTICE_: _Ask caregiver to make large gestures and cooing sounds; _copy baby's sounds and gestures_ to try to elicit smile, and see baby's responses_

_ADVISE for CONCERN that caregiver should try to communicate more with baby._ Advise: A baby and young child begin to understand many words before they can speak, but if we do not talk to our babies they will not learn new words. Children communicate with us from a young age, even before learning to speak they tell us what they need and they know when we are happy or sad.

ADVISE for CONCERN: Playing is important to support healthy development. A baby who has no opportunities to play, and practice new skills will not develop healthily. We need to ensure our babies have lots of opportunities to play, practice new skills, see new things and copy what others do. For example, when they shake a rattle they learn what sound it makes, they learn to move the rattle. Suggest one play that a caregiver can try with the child and instruct them how they can play it with a child. Then emphasize how the child enjoys playing with a caregiver.

At this age, good ways to play with your child are to: (_demonstrate_)
->Provide ways for your child to see, hear, feel, move freely, and touch you. Slowly move colorful things for your child to see and reach for. _Sample toys: shaker rattle, big ring on a string._ ->At this age, children have the tendency of putting everything they get into their mouths. Caregivers should not discourage them from doing this as the mouth is the most sensitive organ at this age which the children use to sense taste, softness, hardness. It is a way of learning. Just make sure that you provide clean and safe items to the child, and that they are medium or large size so the child will not choke.
Looks into baby's eyes and talks softly to baby
Tries to force smile or is not responsive to baby
Responds to baby's sounds and gestures to get baby to smile
ASK: Can you show me how you talk to your baby and how you try to get your baby to smile? Indicate how caregiver interacts with baby
Gets the child's attention with a shaker toy or other object
Moves the baby's arms and legs or gently strokes the child
ASK: Can you show me how you play with your baby? Please demonstrate for me. What do you think your child is learning while they play? Indicate how the mother interacts with the baby:

_PRAISE caregiver for communicating well with baby._ ADVISE: Children communicate with us from a young age, even before learning to speak they tell us what they need and they know when we are happy or sad. By talking to our children and telling them the names of things they recognise words and this helps them learn to talk. Repetition is a very good strategy for a child learning.

PRAISE mother for playing well with baby. Advise: Babies learn by playing and trying things out, and by observing and copying what others do. For example, when they shake a rattle they learn what sound it makes, they learn to move the rattle.
Who does the child usually spend time with?

SAY: I'd like to talk with you about your child's behavior and development and observe how you interact together. This is an important time in their life because the way they will grow and learn, _it depends very much on behaviors that you show to your child_.
SAY: I want to ask you some questions about how your child is learning and also discuss how you can play and communicate with your child to help them best grow and learn.
Welcome caregivers and family members and encourage them to join the discussion
Assess Development and Coach on Play and Communication

_Instructions for CHV_ Select 1 play and 1 communication activity from the Play and Communication Guide in the age range of the child.
->Show the parents the picture and explain the activity. ->Let caregivers try the activity. Observe the interaction and help caregivers be sensitive and responsive to child's cues. Aim to help the caregiver and child have a successful “two-way” interaction with caregiver observing, listening and responding to child. ->If child is not able to do the activity choose from younger age range or suggest ways to make the play simpler. If child is at end of age range and can easily, suggest ways to help the child learn something new or choose activity from next older age range ->Encourage the caregiver to continue to engage in play and communication activities daily.
SAY: Now let's practice some new play and communication activities. These activities will help your child learn through seeing, hearing, touching, moving thinking or feeling.
SAY: Many people may face challenges when it comes to playing and communicating with their child to stimulate child development. I want to talk to you about some common challenges parents face.
_HARSH TREATMENT CONCERN _: _ASK CHV to consider: During the visit, did you have any concerns that child is being treated harshly?_
_LEAVE CHILD CONCERN_: Are you concerned that you have to leave the child with someone else for periods of time?
_SLOW TO LEARN CONCERN_: _Do you or the caregiver have any concerns that child is not responding or seems slow?_
_STRESSFUL FAMILY ENVIRONMENT_: _Is the family environment stressful with a lot of arguments_?
_NO TOYS CONCERN_: Are you concerned That not having toys is a problem?
On how many days in the past week was the child left in the care of another child, that is, someone less than 10 years old, for more than an hour?

Sometimes adults taking care of children have to leave the house to go shopping, wash clothes, or for other reasons and have to leave young children. On how many days in the past week was the child left alone for more than an hour?
I want to ask you some other specific questions about your child's development.
Named, counted, or drew things for the child
Read books or looked at picture books
Sang to or with the child, including lullabies
Took the child outside the home
In the past three days, did the mother or any household member age 15 or over engage in any of the following activities with the child?
_STRESS CONCERN_: Are you concerned about feeling too stressed or burdened to play and communicate with your child?
_TIME CONCERN_: Are you concerned about feeling that you don't have enough time to play and communicate?
Did the partner/male head of household stay for the entire visit?
How many children's books or picture books or cards do you have in your home? _This can be any written material with pictures_
Partner showed no interaction/asked no questions; seemed indifferent about the material
Partner asked minimal questions; seemed somewhat concerned or interested in the material
Partner asked many questions; seemed very concerned and interested in the material
How would you define the partner/male head of household's interaction during the visit?
Does the child play with homemade toys, such as dolls, cars, or other toys made at home?
Does the child play with household objects such as bowls or pots, or objects found outside, such as sticks, rocks, animal shells or leaves?
Does the child play with toys from a shop or manufactured toys?

_If you have concerns that child is not safe due to harsh or physical discipline, refer immediately to the Shehia Women and Children Coordinator or Sheha, who will help you contact the correct authorities or individual. You can also refer to the nearby police station or one stop centre._

Having a child who is slow to learn can be stressful. A child with delays in development may require more help with feeding and with learning new things. Extra time spent in helping a slow to learn child with feeding and with playing and communicating can a child develop new abilities. Thank you for talking about this issues with me today. I noticed a concern that I would advise you see a doctor for. I am going to refer you to the nearest health facility so you can ask them about this concern.
Refer family for hearing and vision screen if necessary.
Compared with other children of the same age, does your child appear in any way mentally backward, dull or slow?
Can your child name at least one object (for example, an animal, a toy, a cup, a spoon)?
Does your child learn to do things like other children his/her age?
Compared with other children, does or did your child any serious delay in sitting, standing or walking?
Does your child have difficulty hearing? (Uses hearing aid, hears with difficulty, completely deaf)
Compared with other children, does your child have difficulty seeing, either in the daytime or at night?
Does your child have difficulty in walking or moving his/her arms, or does he/she have weakness, and/or stiffness in the arms or legs?
Does your child speak at all (can he/she make him or herself understood in words; can say any recognizable words)?
Does your child sometimes have fits, becomes rigid, or lose consciousness?
Is your child's speech in any way different from normal (not clear enough to be understood by people other than the immediate family)?
When you tell your child to do something, does he/she seem to understand what you are saying?

ADVISE FOR CONCERN: Harsh child discipline will not support healthy development.Correcting and teaching the child in a positive manner is more effectivePraise the child for good behavior.Give the child choices about what to do, instead of saying "don't."

->_Needing to leave children with others is also a common challenge that mothers and caregivers face. Identify at least one person (over 10 years old) who can care for the child regularly and give the child love and attention. This individual should be advised on how to appropriately care for the child. _ ->_One challenge if you want your child to get used to another caregiver, is maybe the child doesn't want to leave your side. It only wants to be with you all the time. If you leave your child may cry. This is also very common. But over time, gradually, they can become accustomed to a new person. You just need to give them some time. Don't be afraid. _ ->_Encourage the mother and father to spend time with the child when possible_

->_Listen to the caregivers feeling and help them identify a key person who can share their feelings and help them with their child._ ->_Encourage the caregiver and other family members to support each other in playing and communicating with the child_ ->_Build their confidence by demonstrating their ability to carry out a simple activity_

ADVISE FOR CONCERN:
->Children take their cues from parents. Try to control your own stress and anger. ->Keep children out of the argument. ->Reassure your children that the argument is not their fault and that you love them.

->_Finding enough time is a common problem that many mothers and caregivers face. One strategy you may try, is to play with the child any time you are with them. For example, if you're cooking dinner and the child is with you, you can try to take a short moment to play with your child a little bit._ ->_Another common challenge is that the you might not get enough help from other members of your family. I suggest, that you speak with other family members to tell them that you are feeling overwhelmed by all the work you are doing at home and taking care of the baby. You can try to encourage people in your family/household to take time to play with the baby. _

->_Actually, almost anything can be a toy. You don't need to spend lots of money. You can use any household objects that are clean and safe. You can even use a little bit of fabric/kitambaa in order to make a little toy. Just make sure it is clean and safe. Now, I would like to help you think about what toys we can make just with the materials around us right now. _ ->_Demonstrate how the caregiver can use local materials to make toys for the child. Show examples appropriate to the child's age. If the children are older, encourage the caregiver to develop no/low cost toys with their children._ ->_Play with child. The child will learn by playing with the caregivers and other people. _
Was the partner/male head of household present during the home visit?
Who engages in these activities with the child?
How many total hours did you or any household member over 15 spend playing with the child in the last three days?
ASK: Has the child recently had any convulsions?
The child has a danger sign. Advise the mother to go to any nearby health facility within the next 24 hours so a doctor can look at the child and help.
ASK: Is the child able to drink or breastfeed?
ASK: Is the child lethargic or unconscious?
ASK: Does the child vomit everything?
SAY: No danger signs were identified in your child, but if the child develops any of the signs we discussed, you should go the health facility immediately.

SEMA: Habari mama? Kama tulivyozungumza wakati wa matembeleo yetu yaliyopita kuwa ntakuja kukutembelea kwa ajili ya kujua maendeleo na afya ya mtoto/watoto, na pia kujadiliana na wewe pamoja na familia juu ya malezi na makuzi bora ya mtoto/watoto wako. Nafahamu kuwa unajua jinsi ya kumtunza mtoto wako na kumkuza vizuri, lakini hii ni kama nyongeza tu ya elimu kutokana na ongezeko la maradhi na matatizo mbali mbali yanayoweza kudhoofisha afya na makuzi bora ya mtoto.

Greet the family and invite others to join the conversation as appropriate.
SAY: I'd like to enroll into infant and child services. I'll do a first check-up on their health today, and them return at important times as they grow up to make sure they are healthy and well. Is that OK?

Ask and listen: How is the baby being kept warm? Praise the family if the baby is kept well wrapped and close to the mother, and is bathed with warm water and dried and wrapped immediately. If not, try to solve any problems.
ASK: What positions are you using? How frequently do you breastfeed? How long will the baby feed at one time? LISTEN: _Allow mother to explain her experience breastfeeding soon after birth._

I'd like to talk to you more about breastfeeding.
ASK: have you ever breastfed?
Praise the family if the baby is getting only breast milk.
ASK: How is it going? Do you have any concerns you wish to discuss? _Discuss concerns with mother._
Is the baby well _attached_? _This means the chin is touching the breast, mouth wide open, lower lip turned outward, more areola visible above than below the mouth._
Discuss with the mother why she is not frequently and exclusively breastfeeding. Advise her and problem solve with her.

Help the mother to:
->Sit or lie comfortably. ->Hold the baby's body against hers. ->Have the baby face the breast. ->Hold the breast and touch the baby's cheek with the nipple, so baby turns and opens mouth. ->Fix the baby's mouth on the nipple. Make sure baby is in a comfortable position to feed.

_OBSERVE: If possible, ask the mother to breastfeed and observe. If not possible, ask questions below rather than observing._
->_WASH your hands. OBSERVE the mother breastfeeding. Try to UNDERSTAND the situation._ ->_Do you notice any problems with breastfeeding? Provide guidance based on what you observe. Try to solve any problems you identify or the mother describes. _
Congratulate mother for breastfeeding in a right position.

Help the mother to improve positioning and attachment. Attachment guidance:
->If baby is too sleepy to feed, try to wake him by rubbing body, arms, or legs. ->If baby is still not feeding from the breast after one hour, squeeze the first, yellow fluid from the breast into a clean cup or spoon. Feed this to baby often and in small amounts, making sure baby can swallow it. ->If baby does not feed well within the first 24 hours after birth, see a health worker. ->For a proper attachment of the infant, help the mother to make sure:
->The baby's head and body are in a straight line ->The baby is facing the breast, the baby's nose is opposite her nipple ->Hold the baby's body close to her body ->Support the baby's whole body, not just the neck and shoulders
->To help the baby to attach, advise the mother to:
->Touch the baby's lips with her nipple ->Wait until the baby's mouth is opened wide ->Move the baby quickly onto her breast, aiming the infant's lower lip well below the nipple.
->Look for signs of good attachment and effective suckling (slow, deep sucks, sometimes pausing). If good, praise the mother and advise her to continue doing the same. If the attachment or suckling is not good, try again. Then reassess.
If breast engorgement, express a small amount of breast milk before starting breastfeeding to soften nipple area so that it is easier for the baby to attach.
How long after birth did the mother first put the child to the breast?
Did the child drink or eat any of the following yesterday during the day or the night?

Hygiene is very important during complimentary feeding, advice this to the mother
->Good hygiene is important to avoid diarrhea ->Use a clean spoon or cup to give foods or liquids to your baby. ->Wash your hands with soap and running water frequently.

Let's talk about food Hygiene
->Good hygiene is important to avoid diarrhea ->Use a clean spoon or cup to give foods or liquids to your baby. ->Wash your hands with soap and running water frequently, especially before feeding your children.
SAY: If your baby is no longer being breastfed, please ensure that they are receiving a healthy, nutritious diet.
ASK: Are you still breastfeeding your baby?
SAY: Continue breastfeeding your baby on demand both day and night. This will maintain health and strength as breast milk continues to be the most important part of your baby's diet.

->Try to cook a variety of food for the baby. ->Each week you can add one new food to your child's diet. Feed your baby complementary foods 2 times a day. Give 2 to 3 tastes at each feed. Breastmilk should be your baby's main food at this age. Continue to breastfeed on demand. ->Drinking plain, clean water is a good habit to the baby's health. ->Use a separate plate to feed the baby.

Your baby's umbilical cord stump dries out and eventually falls off -; usually within one to two weeks after birth. In the meantime, treat the area gently:
->_Keep the stump dry._Expose the stump to air to help dry out the base. Keep the front of your baby's diaper folded down to avoid covering the stump. ->_DO NOT apply any substance to the cord._ It may be dangerous to your baby to apply any substances to his or her cord. ->_Stick with sponge baths._While there's no harm in getting the stump wet, sponge baths might make it easier to keep the stump dry ->_Let the stump fall off on its own._Resist the temptation to pull off the stump yourself

You and your partner/ husband or family members are responsible for the infant's brain and mind development, and these early months and years should be used for his/her growth. A newborn baby can see things, feel things, and can respond to the voices and sounds she or he hears. A baby can tell us she or he is hungry or unhappy by the sounds he makes. A baby loves to see faces of her or his mum and dad (or other important caregiver). [see translation in EFFECTS job aids] The more opportunities we give our babies to explore the world (by playing, practicing new skills, seeing new things and copying what others do), the more information is given to the brain. For your newborn baby, you can: [Days 0-7:]
->Talk and Mwimbie mtoto
[Days 8 to month 6]: [use points in image in dropbox: Dropbox (D-tree)\DTree\Country Programs\Tanzania\Projects\Botnar Foundation ZNZ\Technical\Design\App Content\Protocols for Digitization\2019.03.12 Infant-Child\Shortened versions\5. Essential newborn care - shortened.docx]
Is the birthweight written on the child card?
Enter a number between 0.5 and 7 KG
Enter brithweight of the child, in KG
Approximately how large was the baby at birth?
On the way to the health facility
_Look at child card and/or KMC booklet for next scheduled follow-up visit. Advise mother to return to the facility for scheduled follow up visits_
ASK: Have you received small baby services at the hospital?
This is a normal size baby and needs normal care. You will learn about care for the normal baby in later sessions.
This child is a small baby and extra care and attention is needed from both CHV and family. We will talk about some of the special care your baby needs.
is doing well and doesn't need to go to the health facility urgently at this time. You'll return for another checkup when it's time for the next visit.
To address an urgent child danger sign
To address issues around learning and child development
To address an urgent neonatal danger sign
needs to go to the health facility for the following reasons:
To receive specialized care for small babies
To make sure he/she has received the proper vaccines
You've declined to consent for to receive community health services. He/She won't receive any more visits from me.
Encourage the parent to take to the facility as soon as possible to receive care. Let them know that you'll return soon to confirm that they were able to receive the proper care at the facility.
Ask mother to make sure facility staff records vaccinations on Child Health Card.
SAY: Please invite your husband and family so we can explain the importance of vaccinations.

SAY: ->Your baby was born on: ->Your baby needs to get vaccines 6 times. At birth, and then 5 more times. -> Using that date, here is the expected vaccination schedule for your baby.
Date Baby's age Number of vaccinations Types of vaccinations
Birth 2 BCG, bOPV0 (return for BCG in month 3 if no scar visible)
6 weeks 4 bOPV1, DTP-HepB-Hib1, PCVI 1, Rota 1
10 weeks 4 bOPV2, DTP-HepB-Hib2, PCVI 2, Rota 2
14 weeks 4 bOPV3, DTP-HepB-Hib3, PCVI 3, [What's the 4th?]
9 months 1 Surua - Rubella 1
18 months 1 Surua - Rubella 2
SAY: Now I would like to review your child's health card to make sure they have received all their vaccines so far. Do you have your child's health card?
LOOK at the health card. Is the next dose to be given the week 10 dose or week 14 dose?
SAY: Next time you get vaccines, your child will get the IPV vaccine (polio vaccine shot instead of drops). Please don't be worried – it is safe and good for your child to receive these vaccines.
Enter the date that the child received the BCG vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the bOPV0 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the bOPV1 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the bOPV2 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the bOPV3 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the DTP-HepB-Hib1 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the DTP-HepB-Hib2 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the DTP-HepB-Hib3 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the PCVI 1 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the PCVI 2 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the PCVI 3 vaccine
Did the child receive the BCG vaccine?
Did the child receive the bOPV0 vaccine?
Did the child receive the bOPV1 vaccine?
Did the child receive the bOPV2 vaccine?
Did the child receive the bOPV3 vaccine?
Did the child receive the DTP-HepB-Hib1 vaccine?
Did the child receive the DTP-HepB-Hib2 vaccine?
Did the child receive the DTP-HepB-Hib3 vaccine?
Did the child receive the PCVI 1 vaccine?
Did the child receive the PCVI 2 vaccine?
Did the child receive the PCVI 3 vaccine?
Did the child receive the Rota 1 vaccine?
Did the child receive the Rota 2 vaccine?
Did the child receive the Surua - Rubella 1 vaccine?
Did the child receive the Surua - Rubella 2 vaccine?
Enter the date that the child received the Rota 1 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the Rota 2 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the Surua - Rubella 1 vaccine
Enter the date that the child received the Surua - Rubella 2 vaccine
RECORD the vaccinations marked on the health card in the app (vaccinations you might have recorded during a previous visit will not be shown).
Okay, please continue taking care of your child.
Congratulate the mother and remind her of the next vaccination date according to the health card. Remind her how important it is that her child takes ALL the vaccines on time.

SAY: If your baby cries for more than 3 hours or if you are worried about how he looks or feels, go to the health facility immediately. Serious bad reactions very rare, but can happen occasionally as with any medicine.

SAY: After getting a vaccine, there is a small chance your baby may get a fever, rash, or a sore arm or leg where the vaccine was given. Give your child plenty of breastmilk. Usually this is mild and not a problem. If it doesn't go away quickly or if you have any concerns, go to the health facility and follow the advice they give you.
Provide counseling message around importance of vaccinations and refer to facility.
Give advice about the facility vaccination schedule and give an update on the stock availability on the vaccination. Work to ensure that the mother goes on the correct day.
If necessary, remind mother that all the vaccines are important to fully protect her child from diseases like diarrhea, polio, measles, Tuberculosis, pneumonia, and others.
LOOK at the health card. Are all vaccinations up to date?
That facility asked me to return on a different date
The baby is too old to receive that vaccination
Why did the child not receive the vaccine?
ASK: Can you tell me why these vaccines are important? What diseases will they prevent?

The following are very important information for you and your baby:
->It is important to take your baby to a health facility for growth monitoring, Vitamin A supplements and deworming
Contact ID of the logged in user
Facility ID for the parent user
Name of the logged in user
You can get one for free at the health facility!
If you sleep under mosquito net you can protect yourself and your child from getting malaria. Do you have a mosquito net?

Do you know that you and your child are susceptible to malaria? You and your baby are at most risk of malaria. Malaria will continue to be an important danger throughout your child's early years of life.
SAY: Your child is underweight. You should go to the nearest healthcare facility to see a doctor to check on your baby.
Look on child's growth booklet for growth faltering. Enter the range of the graph that the child is within.
Measure MUAC and indicate either green, yellow, or red color
Baby is okay. Praise the caregiver.

SAY: Based on your child's MUAC, he/she is severely malnourished. In order to assist you in providing proper nutrition to your child, I am conducting a referral and will return in a few days to follow up on the referral.

The child is at risk for malnutrition. SAY: Your child is in need of special care and attention to keep from getting malnourished. During the next visit, I will continue discussing best nutritional practices with you.
Palm is not yellow or pale
SAY: I will now check your child for anemia. Is there palm pallor? Compare CHV palm with that of a child.
In order to assist you in providing proper care to your child, I will conduct a referral for anemia.
Your baby has a danger sign. This is an URGENT and super serious emergency. You should take the baby IMMEDIATELY to the nearest facility. If it is closed, you need to go to the closest hospital.
Cord red, bleeding, or draining pus
SAY: I'm now going to screen your baby for neonatal danger signs.
Difficulty or fast breathing (Two counts of 60 breaths or more in one minute)
Excessive crying or restlessness for more than one hour, non-stop
Movement only when stimulated, or no movement even on stimulation
Not able to feed since birth, or stopped feeding well
Yellow or pale palms and soles (hands and feet), and lips. Soles should be red/pink.
A danger sign has been identified. Advise the mother to go to any nearby health facility within the next 24 hours so a doctor can look at the baby and help.
Not passing stool for a day
Not passing urine for a day
No danger signs were identified in your baby, but if the baby develops any of the signs we discussed, you should go the health facility immediately.

Explain the following regarding the importance of breastfeeding a small baby at least every 2-3 hours:
->Breast milk is the best food for a small baby as it protects the baby against infections ->A small baby needs to be fed more often because they can only take very small amounts of breast milk at a time
If a baby is sleeping for longer than 2 hours, wake the baby up by smooth touch on soles such as palms and foot soles to breastfeed, even during the night
ASK: Does your baby breastfeed well?

_Start the discussion by explaining/defining a "small" baby:_
->A small baby is a baby who is born with less than 2000 grams and is considered to be of low birth weight ->A very low birth weight baby weighs less than 1500 grams at birth
I'd like to talk to you more about breastfeeding. ASK: Have you ever breastfed?
Praise the family if the baby is getting only breast milk.

Let's practice the proper attachment of the baby during breastfeeding. Could you please show me how you attach the baby?
->_Help the mother to improve attachment: Ask her to touch the baby's lips with her nipple, wait until the baby's mouth is opening wide, and move the baby quickly onto her breast, aiming his lower lip below the nipple.  _
_Ask the mother to show you how to position the baby during breast feeding_
Discuss with the mother why she is not frequently and exclusively breastfeeding. Advise her and problem solve with her.

SAY: Small babies may have more health issues than other babies. It is important to look for danger signs, keep them warm, and feed them often to keep them healthy. We are going to learn the issues small babies may face and how we can take care of them.
Did the child drink or eat any of the following yesterday during the day or the night?
How long after birth did the mother first put the child to the breast?
ASK: How often do you breastfeed your baby, including night and day time?

Your baby's umbilical cord stump dries out and eventually falls off -; usually within one to two weeks after birth. In the meantime, treat the area gently:
->_Keep the stump dry._Expose the stump to air to help dry out the base. Keep the front of your baby's diaper folded down to avoid covering the stump. ->_DO NOT apply any substance to the cord._ It may be dangerous to your baby to apply any substances to his or her cord. ->_Stick with sponge baths._While there's no harm in getting the stump wet, sponge baths might make it easier to keep the stump dry ->_Let the stump fall off on its own._Resist the temptation to pull off the stump yourself

Advise the mother on maintaining good hygiene, including washing her hands often with soap and water. Explain that preventing infections is even more important for the small baby than the normal baby as the small baby is more prone to infections.
SAY: Kangaroo Mother Care is a method of care for small babies in order to keep the baby warm. The method involves infants being carried with skin-to-skin contact. _Emphasize that anyone can do KMC._

_Review the following steps in placing the baby skin-to-skin, and observe the mother as she practices KMC_
->Undress the baby except for a nappy, hat and socks. ->Place the baby upright between the caregiver's breasts or chest with the baby's chest. ->Put the legs of the baby along the mother's ribs and turn his or her head to one side. ->Secure the baby with a special blouse or cloth tied around the mother and the baby. ->The mother can then wear a shirt, sweater or shawl if she wants. ->Ask the mother to breastfeed the baby as often as the baby wants but at least every 2 hours. ->It is very important that the baby receives the skin to skin contact for 20 hours every day

ASK: Do you know why small babies need to stay extra warm in the first days of life?
->Small babies have less fat than larger babies, so low body temperature can be a major issue. Make sure your baby is dry and warm all the time. ->Kangaroo mother care (KMC) is an excellent way for mothers to keep these babies warm at all times. The skin-to-skin contact of KMC helps keep the baby's body temperature normal.

You and your partner/ husband or family members are responsible for the infant's brain and mind development, and these early months and years should be used for his/her growth. A newborn baby can see things, feel things, and can respond to the voices and sounds she or he hears. A baby can tell us she or he is hungry or unhappy by the sounds he makes. A baby loves to see faces of her or his mum and dad (or other important caregiver). [see translation in EFFECTS job aids] The more opportunities we give our babies to explore the world (by playing, practicing new skills, seeing new things and copying what others do), the more information is given to the brain. For your newborn baby, you can: [Days 0-7:]
->Talk and Mwimbie mtoto
[Days 8 to month 6]: [use points in image in dropbox: Dropbox (D-tree)\DTree\Country Programs\Tanzania\Projects\Botnar Foundation ZNZ\Technical\Design\App Content\Protocols for Digitization\2019.03.12 Infant-Child\Shortened versions\5. Essential newborn care - shortened.docx]
I'd like to discuss disposal of children's feces with you. A child's stool can be dangerous and can cause diseases. ASK: Where does your child defecate? Where do you dispose your child feces?
ASK: Do you have a latrine in your household?
Using latrines can help prevent the spread of disease. If possible, you should have a latrine in your home.

Explain to the mother the dangers of child stool and safe disposal:
->The safest way to dispose of a child's feces is to help the child use a toilet or latrine or, for very young children, to put or rinse their feces into a toilet or latrine. ->There is evidence that children's feces could be riskier than adults' feces ->Children's feces should be treated with the same concern as adult feces, using safe disposal methods that ensure separation from human contact and household contamination

It is very important to wash your hands with soap and water before touching a baby: Newborns can get an infection more easily than an adult or an older childInfection in a newborn can be dangerous because child immunity is still weak/not fully developedFrequent and correct hand washing is one of the most effective ways to prevent infections.

Discuss materials needed to wash hands:
->We need clean flowing water and soap. ->If water and soap is not available, hands can be cleaned with ash instead.

The most important moments to wash hands are:
->after defecation, ->after cleaning a child's bottom, ->before feeding a child, ->before eating food, ->before preparing food, ->After handling raw meat, fish, or poultry.

MSHAURI: Umejitahidi sana kumfariji mtoto wako ila nilikuwa nataka kuongezea kuwa ni muhimu kumtizama mtoto machoni, kuwashika vizuri na kuongea nao muda wote. _Msaidie Mama kuigiza tena na kutumia njia mpya._

MSHAURI Umejitahidi kumuonya mtoto wako kutofanya makosa. Ila kwa kuengezea Ni vyema kama mlezi kuhakikisha mtoto anakuwa na tabia njema. Njia hii ni bora kwa maendeleo ya mtoto kuliko kumtukana au kumpiga. Ikiwa mtoto atafanya jambo jema jaribu kumpongeza ili aendelee na tabia mzuri. _Washawishi wanawake kuonyesha tena kutumia mbinu mpya._
MSHAURI </P Muombe mlezi kuiga mienendo ya mtoto na kufata vile mtoto anavyotaka.

Naomba kujadiliana na wewe njia mzuri ya kuwarekebisha watoto wakiwa wamefanya makosa, kwa mfano jaalia mtoto anataka kuchukua kikombe cha chai moto, hili ni kosa, kwasababu huenda chai ikamwagikia akaungua. Je unaweza kunionesha ni vipi unaweza kumrekebisha mtoto katika hali hii?
Je umemuona mlezi akiwa anamfariji mtoto?
Je umemuona mlezi akimrekebisha mtoto wake?
Hawezi kufariji mtoto na mtoto hamuangalii mlezi kwa ajili ya kutafuta faraja.
Muangalie mtoto machoni ongea na mtoto kwa upole, kwa upole mguse au mshike mtoto kwa ukaribu.
Je ni vipi mlezi alimfariji mtoto wake na kumuonesha upendo?
Muapushe mtoto na vitendo visivyostahiki kwa kutumia vifaa vya kuchezea
Anamjibu mtoto kwa hasira na kumkaripia.
Haendi na mtoto anazuia mwenendo wa mtoto hamjibu mtoto.
anaenda karibu au na kutembea na mtoto, anaongea na mtoto na anamjibu mtoto
_wakati wa tembeleo ni vipi mlezi aliweza kuingiliana na kujibu mienendo, shughuli na milio ya mtoto?_
_Kwa maswali haya yafuatayo, chunguza maingiliano baina ya mlezi na mtoto ikiwa itawezekana. ikiwa hukuweza kuchunguza mpaka unamaliza tembeleo, muombe mlezi akuoneshe._

MPONGEZE: Umefanya vizuri sana kumfariji mtoto na kumuonesha upendo. Unajua sana jinsi ya kuwafariji watoto. kwa mfano ni muhimu sana kumcheki mtoto kwenye macho, kuwashika vizuri, na kuongea nao muda wote.

MSIFIE: Umefanya vizuri jinsi ulivyomuonya mtoto wako kutofanya makosa. Ni vyema kama mlezi kuhakikisha mtoto anakuwa na tabia njema. Njia hii ni bora kwa maendeleo ya mtoto kuliko kumtukana au kumpiga. Ikiwa mtoto atafanya jambo jema jaribu kumpongeza ili aendelee na tabia mzuri.
Ningependa tuongee kuhusu jinsi ya kumfariji mtoto na kuwaonesha upendo. emu tujalie mtoto wako amekereka saa hivi. je unaweza kunionesha vipi ungelimfanyia ili kuwafariji, na kuwaonesha upendo?
Je umri wa mtoto unaendana na maendeleo yake?

Taabia za watoto kulingana na umri wao > Miezi 3 hadi 6 ->Husikiza milio ->Hutazama na kufatiza macho kwenye vitu ->Anapenda kuangalia watu usoni ->hutaka kuvishika vitu -> Miezi 6 hadi 9 ->Unapojaribu kutabasamu na yeye anatabasamua ->Huonesha ishara kama ana njaa -> Anapiga kelele ili kuwasilina -> anaweza kukaa kitako kwenyewe -> huwa anamtambua na anaitika kwa mlezi wake na rangi
> Miezi 9 hadi 12: > Anaanza kuzungumza na kuunda maneno ya kwanza -> Anajibu kwa walezi kwa maneno na kimwili -> anaanza kutambaa -> Anasimama mwenyewe -> Kutembea mwenyewe au kwa msaada ->12 months up to 2 years: -> Kutembea mwenyewe -> Majadiliano, na umri wa miaka 2 huanza kuunda sentensi fupi sana -> Anaanza kuunda maswali -> Anakula mwenyewe -> Kulala usiku mzima
> Miaka 2 au Zaidi:
Anaanza kueleza anachokitaka, unaonesha vitu anavyovipenda, kuuliza maswali na kadhalika, anaweza kutunga sentensi mzima na anaanza kuhoji mambo yanayouwazunguka.

ULIZA: Je kuna wanafamilia wengine au walezi ili washiriki katika mjadala huu unaohusu maendeleo ya mtoto Hakikisha unamuhamisha baba kushiriki kama yupo, wakaribishe ili washiriki kwenye mjadala, pia muombe mama awe na mtoto wake karibu.

USHAURI kuwasiliana vizuri na mtoto wako ni muhimu sana kwani inakuza uhusiano bora na maendeleo ya afya ya mtoto wako. Kupitia kuzungumzo na watoto na kuwaambia majina ya vitu, inawafanya wayajue maneno na hili linasaidia wao kuweza kuongea kwa urahisi.

USHAURI. Kucheza kuna umuhimu mkubwa kwa mtoto. Mtoto asiyepata fursa ya kucheza hatakuwa na maendeleo mazuri ya afya. Watoto wanajifunza kupitia kuona wanachofanya watu wengine, huiga na kufanya mambo mapya. Michezo ni njia bora ya kuwasaidia watoto kujifunza na kufanya mambo mapya.
Haongei na mtoto au anaongea kwa ukali na mtoto
Muangalie mtoto machoni n uongee nae kwa upole sana, uliza maswali
Anajaribu kulazimisha tabasamu au hamfanyii kazi mtoto.
je unaweza kunionesha vipi unazungumza na mtoto wako?. Je unaweza kuonesha unawafanyia nini ukitaka watabasamu?

ULIZA: Ni vipi unacheza na mtoto wako? Je unaweza kunionesha kikawaida jinsi unavyocheza na mtoto wako? onesha kama njia mlezi anayocheza na mtoto kwa mfano kwa tumia toiz, michezo ya maneno au nyenginezo inayoendana na umri wake.

MPONGEZE Hivi ni vizuri sana, kuwasiliana na mtoto wako kama hivi itakuza maendeleo ya afya ya mtoto wako. Kupitia kuzungumza na watoto na kuwaambia majina ya vitu, inawafanya wayajue maneno na hili linasaidia wao kuweza kuongea kwa urahisi.

Mpongeze mlezi analionesha jinsi anavyocheza na mtoto kwa maendeleo ya afya yao Mshauri: Watoto wanajifunza kupitia walichokiona kutoka kwa wengine, huiga na kujifunza vitu vipya. kucheza ni njia mzuri ya kumsaidia mtoto kujifunza and kufanya vitu vipya! Kadiri tunavyotoa fursa watoto wetu kucheza ndio akili zao hupata taarifa na uelewa mpana. Kwa mfano mtoto anavyotikisa kengele anajifunza mlio unaojitokeza, wanajifunza jinsi ya kuizungusha kengele, pia inamsaidia kuwa imara mwilini. Muache mtoto akuoneshe mchezo gani anapenda kucheza na wewe usiwe wa kwanza kumuamulia vipi acheze
Muitikie mtoto kulingana na sauti yake na anavyotaka. Muite jina lake na mtizame jinsi anavyojibu
Njia mzuri za kuwasiliana na mtoto wako kwa umri huu ni:
Muulize mtoto wako maswali rahisi. Mjibu mtoto wako kadiri anavyojaribu kuzungumza muoneshe na zungumza huhusu asili, picha na vitu

Muhimize mtoto wako kuzungumza na jibu maswali ya mtoto wako. Wafunze watoto wako hadithi, nyimbo na michezo. Ongea kuhusu picha au vitabu. Watoto watasoma maneno kama utachora kitu alafu utalirejea neno. Mshauri mlezi kwamba wanaweza kuchora picha mzuri kwenye madaftari ya watoto wenyewe, sio lazima wanunue dukani. Kwa mfano: Kitabu cha picha mambo ya kuchora ,kengele, pembea, baskeli
Mwambie mtoto wako majina ya vitu na watu. Muoneshe mtoto wako jinsi ya kutumia maumbile ya mwili wakati anazungumza kwa mfano akisema "kwa heri" Sampuli ya Toiz mdoli.
kucheza ni muhimu ili kuwezesha maendeleo ya afya. Kwa umri huu, njia mzuri ya kucheza na mtoto wako ni:
Msaidie mtoto wako hesabu, kujua majina ya vitu na kulinganisha vitu. Mfanyie toiz mwanao . Sampuli ya toiz: vitu vyenye rangi na maumbo tofauti Bakora.ili achague,
Mpatie mtoto wako kitu kisafi na salama ashike, kengele na chupa. Sampuli ya toiz: Vibakuli na mifuniko yake, Sufuria na Kijiko.
Ifiche Toiz ambayo mtoto anaipenda chini ya nguo au boksi, halafu uangalie kama anaweza kuifichua Play peek-a-boo
Mpe vitu mtoto apange,pia aviweke kwenye kontena na kisha avitowe

Njia mzuri ya kuwasiliana na mtoto wako katika umri huu: (onesha kwa vitendo) Tabasamu na cheka na mtoto wako. Ongea na mtoto wako. Fatilia mazungumzo kwa kujaribu kuiga sauti na jinsi mtoto anavyotumia lugha za ishara za mwili wake _FANYA_:_ Muombe Mlezi kuigiza lugha za ishara na sauti; kuigiza sauti ya mtoto na lugha ya ishara_ajaribu kutabasamu na kuona mtoto atafanyaje.

_MSHAURI mlezi kwamba ajaribu kuzungumza na mtoto sana._ Mshauri: watoto huanza kutambua maneno mengi kabala hawajaanza kuongea, lakini kama hatutoongea na watoto wetu hawataweza kujifunza maneno mapya. Watoto huwa wanawasiliana na sisi wakiwa wachanga sana, na hata kabla hawajaanza kuongea huwa wanatwambia wanachohitaji na pia wanajua muda tukiwa tumefurahi au tumekasirika .

USHAURI Kucheza ni muhimu sana kwa maendeleo ya afya ya mtoto. Mtoto asiye na fursa ya kucheza na kujifunza mambo mapya ahakuwa na maendeleo bora ya afya. Tunapaswa kuhakikisha watoto wetu wanapata fursa ya kutosha ya kucheza, kujifunza kufanya mambo mapya, kuona vitu vipya na kuiga watu wengine wanachokifanya. Kwamfano wanapotikisa kengele hujifunza ile sauti inayotoka wakati unapoitikisa kengele, pia hujifunza jinsi ya kuizungusha kengele. Pendekeza mchezo mmoja ambao mlezi anaweza kujaribu kucheza na mtoto wake na waelekeze jinsi kuucheza na mtoto. Kisha sisitiza jinsi mtoto anavyofurahi pale anapochezana mlezi wake.

Kwa umri huu, njia mzuri ya kucheza na mtoto ni:( _Onesha _) ->Andaa njia za mtoto wako kuona, kusikia, kuhisi, kutembea kwa uhuru, na kukugusa. Jaribu kuhamisha polepole vitu anavyovipenda mtoto wako ili avione na kuvifikia. _ Mfano wa toiz: kengele, pete kubwa iliyofungwa kwenye kamba._ ->Katika umri huu watoto wana tabia ya kuingiza mdomoni kila wanachokipata. Walezi wasiwakataze kufanya hivyo kwasababu mdogo ndio kiungo kikubwa cha hisia kwao kipindi hiki, ambacho wanatukimia kuonja ladha, ulaini na ugumu wa kitu. Ni njia ya kujifunza. Cha msingi ni kuhakikisha unampatia mtoto kitu kilicho salama na safi na kiwe kikubwa ili kuhakikisha hawakimezi
Muangalie mtoto machoni na uongee nae kwa upole sana
Anajaribu kulazimisha tabasamu au hamjibu mtoto.
Fanyia kazi sauti na vitendo vya mtoto ili kupata tabasamu la mtoto
ULIZA: Je unaweza kunionesha jinsi unavyoongea na mwanao au kumfanya atabasamu Onesha jinsi mlezi anavyoingiliana na mtoto.
Mfanye mtoto ashughulike nawe kwa kutumia cherewa au kitu chengine cha kuchezea.
Chezesha miguu na mikono ya mtoto au mpige pige mtoto kwa upole sana

ULIZA: Je unaweza ukanionesha jinsi unavyocheza na mtoto wako?, naomba nioneshe tafadhali. unafikri watoto wako wanajifunza kitu gani wakati mkicheza?. onesha jinsi mama anavyoingiliana na nmtoto wake?

Mpongeze mlezi kwa kuwasiliana vizuri na mtoto._ MSHAURI: Watoto huwa wanawasiliana na sisi hata pale wanapokuwa katika umri mdogo. hata kabla hawajaanza kuongea huwa wanatwambia wanachohitaji na pia wanajua nyakati tukiwa tumefurahi au tumekasirika . Kupitia kuzungumza na watoto wetu na kuwaambia majina ya vitu kunawafanya wayatambue majina na hili linawasidia kueza kuongea kwa urahisi. Kurejea rejea ni njia ya mzuri ya toto kujifunza.

MPONGEZE mama kwa jinsi alivyocheza na mtoto wake vizuri. Mshauri: Mtoto anajifunza kupitia michezo na hujaribu kufanya baadhi ya vitu. Huchunguza na kuiga kile watu wanachokifanya kwamfano wakitikisa kengele hujifunza sauti inayotoka kwenye kengele pia hujifunza kuizungusha kengele.

SEMA: Ningelipenda kuongea na nyinyi kuhusu tabia na maendeleo ya mtoto wenu, chunguza jinsi wanavyofatilia mazungumzo yako. Hizi ni nyakati muhimu sana kwa watoto katika maisha yao, kipindi hiki watoto ndio hujifunza hivyo inategemea na matendo yenu kwani mnachokifanya ndio watachojifunza
SEMA ningependa kukuuliza maswali kuhusu jinsi mtoto wako anavyojifunza na kujadili jinsi ya kucheza na kuwasilina na mtoto wako ili kuwasaidia wakuwe vyema na waweze kujifunza.
wakaribishe walezi na familia na wahamasishe kushiriki kwenye mjadala
Tathmini maendeleo na mafunzo kuhusu michezo na mawasiliano.

_Maelekezo kwa CHV_ Chagua mchezo 1 na shughuli moja ya mawasiliano kutoka kwenye muongoz wa michezo na mawasiliano kulingana na umri wa mtoto. -> Waoneshe wazee picha na waelezee shughuli ->Wape nafasi walezi wafanye shughuli. Chunguza maingiliano na wasaidie walezi wawe wanafatilia mienendo ya mtoto. Dhamiria kuwasaidia walezi na mtoto kuwa na mafanikio ya maingiliano ya pande mbili jinsi mlezi akiangalia, kusikiliza na kumjibu mtoto. ->Ikiwa mtoto hawezi kufanya shughuli yoyote chagua mchezo unaoendana na umri wa mtoto au pendekeza njia ya kufanya mchezo uwe rahisi. Ikiwa mtoto ni mkubwa kidogo na anaweza kwa urahisi, pendekeza njia za kumsaidia mtoto kujifunza kitu kipya au chagua shughuli kutoka kwa umri mkubwa kidogo unaolingana na yeye. ->Muhimize mlezi kuendelea na mchezo na shughuli za mawasiliano
SEMA: wacha tujifunze michezo mipya na njia nyengine za kuwasiliana. mambo haya yatamsaidia mtoto wako kwenye uoni, uskikivu, kugusa, kubadilisha mawazo au hisia.

SEMA: Watu wengi hupata changamoto wakati wanapocheza na kuwasiliana na watoto wao, ili kuhamasisha maendeleo ya mtoto. Ningependa nizungumze nawe kuhusu baadhi ya changamoto maarufu ambazo wazee zinawakuta.
_ADHABU MBAYA_:_ Mtake CHV azingatie: wakati wa tembeleo lako je uliona hali yoyote inayoonesha mtoto analelewa vibaya au ukali?_
TATIZO LA KUONDOKA_: Je unakwazwa na hali ya wewe kuondoka na kumuacha mtoto kwa mtu mwengine kwa kipindi fulani?
_TATIZO LA UZITO WA KUJIFUNZA_ Je! Wewe au mlezi ana matatizo yoyote kwamba mtoto hajibu au inaonekana kuwa mgumu wa kujifunza?
_HALI MIGOGORO YA KIFAMILIA_: Je mazingira ya familia ni yenye malumbano na yenye kusabisha msongo wa mawazo?
_TATIZO LA KUTOKUWA NA TOIZ_: Je unahisi kutokuwa na toiz ni tatizo?
Ni siku ngapi katika wiki iliyopita mtoto aliachiwa mtoto mwenziwe aliye chini ya miaka 10 amlee kwa zaidi ya saa moja?

Baadhi ya mda walezi huondoka nyumbani na kwenda dukani, kuosha nguo au kwa sababu nyengine btofauti na ikabidi kuwaacha watoto. Ni siku ngapi nyuma mtoto aliachwa peke yake nyumbani kwa zaidi ya saa moja?
Naomba nikuulize baadhi ya maswali maalumu yanayohusu maendeleo ya mtoto wako
Mtoto ametajiwa, amehesabiwa, amechorewa vitu kwa ajili yake.
Hakuna kimoja wapo kati ya hivyo.
kusoma vitabu au kuangalia picha za vitabu
Siku tatu zilizopita nyuma, Je mama au mwanafamilia yoyote aliye na umri wa miaka 15 au zaidi amefanya akiwa na mtoto shughuli hizi zifuatazo?
_TILIA MKAZO_ Je huwa unajiskia vibaya au mwenye dhima kucheza na kuwasiliana na mtoto wako?
_TATIZO LA MUDA_ Je unahisi huna muda wa kutosha wakucheza na kuwasiliana?
Je Mwenza/Mkuu wakaya alikaa mda wote wa temebeleo lako?
Je nyumbani kwako kuna vitabu vingapi vya watoto au vitabu vyenye picha au kadi?_
Mwenza hakuonesha ushirikiano, hakuuliza maswali au anaonekana hayaafiki maelezo.
Mwenza ameuliza maswali machache na ameonekana kuzingatia na kufurahishwa na maelezo.
Mwenza ameuliza maswali mengi na ameonekana kuzingatia na kufurahishwa na maelezo.
Kivipi unamuona wenza/ mkuu wa kaya alivyoshiriki katika mazungumzo yenu wakati wa tembeleo lako?
Je mtoto huwa anachezea na toiz zilizotengezwa nyumbani kama vile midoli, magari au toiz nyengine zinazotengezwa nyumbani.
Je mtoto anacheza na vitu vinavyopatikana nyumbani kama vile upawa, masufuria au vitu anavyoukutana navyo nje kama vile bakora, vipuri vya wanyama au majani?
Je mtoto anachezea toiz kutoka madukani au zilizosanifiwa?

Ikiwa una shaka kwamba huenda mtoto hayupo salama kutokana na adhabu kali mpe rufaa haraka iwezekanavyo kwa Mratibu wa Wanawake na Watoto wa Shehia au Sheha ambaye atakusaidia kuwasiliana na mamlaka sahihi au mtu sahihi. Pia unaweza kumpeleka kituo cha police kilichopo karibu nawe au kituo cha mkono kwa mkono.

Kuwa na mtoto ambaye ni mzito wa kujifunza kunaweza kukufanya ujiskie vibaya. Mtoto anayechelewa katika maendeleo anahitaji msaada zaidi wa kulishwa na kujifunza mambo mapya. Muda wa ziada unahitajika kumsaidia kula, kucheza naye na kuwasiliana naye ili mtoto aweze kupata uwezo mpya. Asante kwa kueza kuzungumza na mimi leo kuhusu mambo haya, nimegundua tatizo ambalo nakushauri uwende kumuona daktari ili akusaidie, nitakupa rufaa uende kituo cha karibu nawe.
Ipe rufaa familia kwenye vipimo vya usikivu na uoni kama kuna hitajio
Ukilinaganisha na watoto wengine je mtoto wako anaonekana ni mzito kiakili, si mchangamfu au ni mgumu kujifunza?
Je mtoto wako anaweza kuita jina la kitu japo kimoja (kwa mfano, mnyama, toiz, kikombe au kijiko)?
Je mtoto wako anajifunza kufanya baadhi vitu kama vile wanavyofanya watoto wa umri wake
Ukilinganisha na watoto wengine je mtoto wako ana tatizo lolote linahusiana na kuchelewa kukaa, kusimama au kutembea?
Je mtoto wako ana tatizo la kusikia( anatumia kifaa kinachomsaidia kusikia, anasikia kwa shida, hasikii kabisa)
Ukilinganisha na watoto wengine je mtoto wako ana tatizo la uoni nyakati za usiku au machana?.
Je mtoto wako anapata shida wakati wa kutembea au anapojaribu kuzungusha mikono yake au Je ana ushaifu wowote kwenye miguu na mikono?
Je mtoto wako anaweza kuzungumza Je anaweza kuzungumza maneno yanayoeleweka?
Je! Mtoto wako wakati mwingine anafaa, inakuwa ngumu, au kupoteza fahamu?
Je mtoto wako anazungumza tofauti na inavyopaswa kuwa (mazungumzo yake hayaeleweki vizuri na watu wengine isipokuwa watu wa karibu wa familia yake)?
Unapomuambia mtoto wako afanye kitu fulani je anaonekana anakuelewa ulichomwambia?

TOA USHAURI Adhabu kali kwa mtoto hazitamsaidia kuwa na maendeleo mazuri ya afya. kuwarekebisha na kuwafundisha watoto kwa namna mzuri ni kitu kinachoshauriwa zaidi. Msifie mtoto akifanya jambo zuri mpe uhuru mtoto achague lipi la kufanya badala ya kusema "usifanye''

>_Hali ya kuwaacha watoto kwa mtu mwengine pia ni moja ya changamoto inayowakumba mama na walezi wengi, orodhesha angalau mtu mmoja (aliye na umri wa zaidi ya miaka 10) ambaye atakuwa anawatunza watoto wako, kuwaandalia na kuwapa upendo. Mtu huyu ni lazima ashauri njia mzuri ya kuwatunza watoto._ ->_Mmoja kati ya changamoto unazoweza kupata ikiwa mtoto wako analelewa na mlezi mwengine na mtoto hataki kukaa mbali nawe, kila mda anapenda awe na wewe tu, kama ukimuacha mtoto wako atalia hii sana huwa linajitokeza. Ila itafika mda watoto watomzoea mlezi wao mpya, unachopaswa ufanya kuwa na subra. Wala usiogope._ ->_Washajihishe mama na baba watenge muda wa kucheza na mtoto ikiwa kuna uwezekano_

Msikilize mlezi jinsi anavyojiskia na msaidie kuorodhesha mtu muhimu ambaye anaweza kumsimulia hisia zake na kumsaidia mtoto wake ->_ Muhamasishe mlezi na ndugu wa familia wawe pamoja kumsaidia mama kucheza na kuwasiliana na mtoto_ ->_ Wajengee uwezo/Imani kwa kuwaonesha uwezo walio nao kufanikisha jambo dogo

TOA USHAURI Watoto huiga matendo ya wazee. Jaribu kupunguza hasira na msongo wa mawazo. Waepushe watoto na malumbano na migogoro? Jaribu kuwahakikishia watoto kuwa migogoro na malumbano yanayojitokeza siyo makossa yao na unawapenda sana.

->_Tatizo la Kuwa muda wakutosha ni kitu ambacho mama na walezi wengi kinawakuta, njia moja unaweza kujaribu ni kucheza na mtoto muda wowote unapokuwa naye, kwamfano ukiwa unapika mlo wa usiku na mtoto akiwa yupo karibu nawe, unaweza kujaribu kutenga mda kidogo kucheza na mtoto wako. ->_Chanagmoto nyengine ni kuwa unaweza kujikuta huna muda wa kutosha wa kuwasaidia wanafamilia, Nashauri ni vyema ukazungumza na wanafamilia waambie kuwa unajikuta umelemewa majukumu mengi nyumbani na kumshughulikia mtoto. Unaweza kujaribu kuwahamasisha wanafamilia kutenga mda kucheza na mtoto.

->_ Ukweli ni kwamba karibu ya kitu kinaweza kuwa toiz. Siyo lazima utumie pesa nyingi kununa toiz. Unaweza kutumia baadhi ya vitu vinavyopatikana majumbani ambavyo ni visafi na salama. Unaweza kutumia kitambaa kwa ajili ya kutengeza toiz. La msingi hakikisha ni safi na salama. Sasa ningependelea nikusaidie kufikiri ni toiz zipi tunaweza kutengeneza kwa kutumia mambo tuliyonayo nyumbani. ->_ Waoneshe ni jinsi gani mlezi anaweza kutumia mambo yaliyopo nyumbani kutengenza toizi kwa ajili ya mtoto. Unesha mfano unaolingana na umri wa mtoto. Ikiwa mtoto ni mkubwa wahamasishe walezi wawatengenezee toiz zisizo na gharama kubwa ->_ Cheza na mtoto. Mtoto atajifunza kucheza na mlezi na watu wengine
Je Mume au Mwenza wake ambaye ndio mkuu wa kaya alikuwepo wakati wa tembeleo lako
Je ni nani huwa anafanya shughuli hizi na mtoto?
Ni jumla ya masaa mangapi umetumia wewe au mwanafamilia yoyote aliye na zaidi ya mika 15 kucheza na mtoto kipindi cha siku tatu nyuma?
Mtoto anaonesha kuwa ana dalili za hatari. Mshauri mama aende kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu naye ndani ya saa 24 ili daktari amuone mtoto na kumsaidia.
ULIZA: Je mtoto anaweza kunywa au kunyonya maziwa ya mama?
Je mtoto analegea na kupoteza fahamu?
ULIZA: Je mtoto anatapika kila kitu?

SEMA: Hakuna dalili ya hatari yoyote iliyoonekana kwa mtoto wako, lakini ikitokea mtoto ameonesha dalili yoyote ya hatari tulizojadili, hakikisha unamuwahisha kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu nawe haraka.

SEMA: Habari mama? Kama tulivyozungumza wakati wa matembeleo yetu yaliyopita kuwa ntakuja kukutembelea kwa ajili ya kujua maendeleo na afya ya mtoto/watoto, na pia kujadiliana na wewe pamoja na familia juu ya malezi na makuzi bora ya mtoto/watoto wako. Nafahamu kuwa unajua jinsi ya kumtunza mtoto wako na kumkuza vizuri, lakini hii ni kama nyongeza tu ya elimu kutokana na ongezeko la maradhi na matatizo mbali mbali yanayoweza kudhoofisha afya na makuzi bora ya mtoto.

Isalimie familia na waalike wengine waliopo washiriki kwenye mazungumzo ikiwa inahitajika. SEMA: Ningependa nimsajili mtoto kwenye huduma za afya za mtoto, leo nitawafanyia vipimo kuhusu afya yao, na nitakuwa narudia kuwatembelea kadiri wanavyoendelea kukuwa ili kuhakikisha kuwa wanakuwa na afya bora. Je mnakubaliana na hili?

Uliza na Sikiliza: Je vipi mtoto huhifadhiwa katika hali ya joto? Mpongeze mlezi ikiwa mtoto huwa anafungwa vizuri na kuwa karibu na mama yake, na ikiwa anakoshwa kwa maji ya vuguvugu na kukaushwa haraka. Ikiwa sio, jaribu kutatua tatizo.

ULIZA: Je unamuekaje mtoto ukitaka kumnyonyesha? Unanyonyesha kila baada ya kipindi gani? mtoto anachukua muda gani kunyonya kwa mara moja? SIKILIZA:_Mruhusu mama kueleza uzoefu wake wa kunyonyesha punde baada ya iujifungua._
Ningependa kuzungumza na wewe kuhusu Kunyonyesha ULIZA: Je ushawahi kunyonyesha?
Ipongeze familia ikiwa mtoto ananyonyeshwa maziwa ya mama peke yake
Msaidie mama jinsi ya kumnyonya vizuri mtoto wake
ULIZA: Je unaendeleaje na kunyonyesha? Je una tatizo lolote unahitaji tujadili? _Jadili tatizo na mama_

Je mtoto amewekwa vizuri kwaijli ya kunyonya? Hii inamaanisha kidevu kinagusa ziwa la mama, mdomo ameufungua vizuri, mdomo wa chini uko nje, Ncha ya ziwa inayoonekana ipo kwa juu kuliko chini ya kinywa.
Je mama amemuweka vizuri mtoto wake wakati anamnyonyesha?
Jadiliana na mama kwanini hamnyonyeshi mtoto wake mara kwa mara na kama inavyopswa . Mshauri na kutatua tatizo.

Msaidie Mama kufanya haya yafuatayo: Kukaa au kulala vizuri Amkamate mtoto vizuri karibu na yeye/Ampakate vizuri Mtoto atazame ziwa la mama Alishike ziwa na mgusishe mtoto nalo kwenye mdomo wake ili mtoto afungue mdomo Hakikisha mdomo wa mtoto umekaa vizuri kwenye ncha ya ziwa la mama. Hakikisha mtoto amewekwa vyema ili kuweza kunyonya vizuri

CHUNGUZA: kama kuna uwezekano muombe mama amnyonyeshe mtoto wake na chunguza, ikiwa hakuna uwezekano muulize mama maswali kuliko kumchunguza. ->_Osha mikono yako. CHUNGUZA je mama anayenyonyesha anaelewa hili ->_Je umegundua kuna tatizo lolote kwenye kunyonyesha? Toa muongozo kwa kile ulichokiona. Jaribu kutatua tatizo lolote uliloliona au alilolisema mama. _
Mpongeze mama kwa kumuweka vizuri mtoto wake wakati wa kumnyonyesha.
Ilichukua muda gani baada ya mtoto kuzaliwa mama alimnyonyesha mtoto?

Usafi ni muhimu wakati wa chakula cha ziada, mshauri hivi mama.-> Tumia kjiko na kikombe safi wakati wa kumlisha na kumnywesha mtoto.->Kosha mikono yako kwa sabuni na maji yanaotiririka mara kwa mara.

Sasa naomba tuongee kuhusu usafi wa chakula. ->Usafi ni mzuri na muhimu kwa kujikinga na maradhi ya kuharisha.->Tumia kijiko au kikombe safi wakati wa kumlisha au kumnywesha mtoto.->Kosha mikono yako kwa sabuni na maji yanayotiririka mara kwa mara, hasa kabla ya kuanza kumlisha mtoto.
SEMA:Ikiwa mtoto wako hanyonyi tena , tafadhali hakikisha anapata chakula chenye afya na kilicho bora.
ULIZA: Je bado unamnyonyesha mtoto wako?

SEMA: Endeleae kumnyonyesha mtoto wako kwa mujibu wa mahitaji yake muda wote, usiku na mchana. Hili litafanya afya ya mtoto kutengemaa na kumfanya awe na nguvu, kwani maziwa ya mama ni chakula muhimu sana kwa mtoto.

>Jaribu kupika vyakula tofauti tofauti kwa ajili ya mtoto.->Kila wiki unaweza kuongeza aina mpya ya chakula kwa mtoto . Mlishe mtoto chakula cha ziada mara mbili kwa siku. Mpe mtoto tastes mara mbili au tatu kwenye kila mlo. Maziwa ya mama yabaki kuwa ni chakula kikuu kwenye umri huu wa mtoto. Endelea kunyonyesha kwa mujibu wa mahitaji ya mtoto->Kunywa maji masafi na ya kawaida ni kitu kizuri kwa afya ya mtoto. . -->Mlishe mtoto kwenye chombo cha peke yake.

Kikawaida Kamba iliyopo kwenye kitovu cha mtoto wako hukauka na kudondoka nyenyewe ndani ya wiki moja ua mbili baada ya mtoto kuzaliwa. Katika kipindi hiki hakikisha unakitunza kipaswavyo-; ->_Hakikisha kinakuwa kikavu: jaribu kukiruhusu kipate hewa ili iwe rahisi kukauka. Unapomvunga mtoto wako hakikisha unamvunga kwa chini ya kitovu ili kuepuka kukifunika. ->_Usiingize kitu chochote kwenye kitovu cha mtoto_unapoeka kitu chochote inaweza kusababisha madhara kwa mtoto. ->_Muogeshe kwa kumfuta ingawa hakuna madhara unapomgesha na ukaakiroesha kitovu ila njia ya kumuogesha kwa kumfuta ni mzuri itasaidia kuafanya kitovu kikauke haraka. ->_Iache kamba ya kitovu idondoke yenyewe epuka kujaribu kukivuta ili kidondoke
Huduma muhimu kwa watoto wachanga: Jinsi ya kukitunza kitovu.

Wewe na mwenzako/ mume wako au familia muna jukumu la kuhakikisha maendeleo ya akili ya mtoto na katika kipindi hiki cha miezi na miaka ya mwanzoni ni vyema kutumiwa vyema ili kuifanya akili yake ikuwe vizuri Mtoto mchanga anaweza kuona vitu, kuvihisi, na anaweza kujibu sauti na milio huwa anasikia. Mtoto anaweza kutuambia kuwa anaona njaa au hana furaha kwa kwa sauti yake. Mtoto huwa anapenda kuona sura ya mama na baba yake (walezi wake wa karibu.)[ angalia tafsiri kwenye muongozo wa kazi] Kadiri tunavyowapa nafasi watoto wetu kudadilisi wanayaona (kwa kucheza, kufanya mambo mapya, kuona vitu vipya na kuiga) ndio kadiri tunavyoweza kuwafanya wawe na akili. Je umepatiwa huduma maalumu wanayopatiwa watoto waliozaliwa wadogo hospitali?
Huu ni ukubwa wa kawaida wa mtoto kwahivyo anahitaji apate uangalizi wa kawaida. Utajifaunza ni jinsi gani tunapaswa kutunza watoto waliozaliwa na ukubwa wa kawaida baadae.
Mpe rufaa kwenye wodi ya watoto njiti
Mtoto huyu amezaliwa mdogo hivyo anahitaji uangalizi wa ziada kutoka kwa wahudumu wa afya wa jamii wa kujitolea na familia. Tutazungumza kuhusu uangalizi maalumu anaopaswa kupatiwa mtoto wako.
Muombe mama ahakikishe kuwa wagudumu wa kituo cha afya wanaandika rikodi kuhusu chanjo kwenye kadi la afya la mtoto.
SEMA: Naomba uniitie Mumeo na wanafamilia ili nipate kuwaeleza umuhimu wa chanjo.
SEMA:->Mtoto wako amezaliwa: ->Mtoto wako anahitaji kupata chanjo mara 6. Wakati wa kuzaliwa, na baada ya hapo mara 5.->Kwa kutumia tarehe, Ufuatao ndio mpangilio wa kupata chanjo kwa mtoto wako.

INGIZA chanjo zilizoanishwa kwenye kadi ya mtoto ya kliniki ndani ya kitendea kazi chako (chanjo ambazo unaweza kuwa umeingiza wakati wa tembeleo lililopita hazitooneshwa).
Sawa, taafadhali hakikisha unaendelea kumtunza mtoto wako vizuri
Mpongeze mama na mkumbushe kuhusu tarehe ya chanjo nyengine kama kadi ilinavyoonesha. mkumbushe kuwa kuna umuhimu mkubwa ikiwa mtoto wake atapatiwa chanjo zote kwa muda uliopangwa.

Ikiwa mtoto wako analia kwa zaidi ya masaa 3 au ukiwa unahofu jinsi anavyooneka au anavyojiskia, nenda kituo cha afya haraka. Ni mara chache sana kujitokeza kwa athari yoyote mbaya. lakini inaweza kujitokeza baadhi muda mtu anapotumia dawa yoyote ile.

SEMA: Baada ya kupata chanjo, mtoto wako anaweza kupata homa, upele au maumivu kwenye mkono au mguu katika maeneo ambayo amechomwa sindano. Mnyonyeshe mtoto wako. Hii ni hali ya nikawaida kujitokeza wala sio tatizo ikiwa hali hii itachelewa kuondoka au una shaka yoyote nenda kituo cha afya na ufate ushauri wataokupatia.
Toa ushauri nasaha kuhusu umuhimu wa chanjo na mpe rufaa aende kituo cha afya.
Washauri kuhusu ratiba ya chanjo kituoni na wape taarifa kuhusu upatikanaji wake, jitahidi uhakikishe kuwa mama anahudhuria kwenye chanjo siku iliyopangwa.
Ikiwa kuna umuhimu, mkumbushe mama kuwa chanjo zote ni muhimu kwa ajiili ya kumkinga mtoto na maradhi kama vile kuhara, polio, like diarrhea, polio, measles, Kifua kikuu, nimonia, na mengineyo.
Nimeambiwa na wahudumu nirudi tarehe nyengine
Mtoto ni mkubwa mno kwa chanjo hiyo
ULIZA: Unaweza kunieleza kwa nini chanjo ni muhimu?Zinakinga maradhi gani?
Taarifa zifuatazo ni muhimu sana kwako wewe na mtoto: Ni muhimu sana kumuwahisha mtoto wako kituo cha afya ili kucheki makuzi yake, kuapata dawa za Vitamin A na dawa za minyoo
Unaweza kupata chandarua kimoja bure kituo cha afya.

SEMA: Mtoto wako ana uzito pungufu hakikisha unamuwahisha kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu na wewe ukamuone daktari ili ampime mtoto wako?
kati ya chini ya 2 na chini ya 3

SEMA: kutokana na kipimo cha lishe cha mtoto wako kinaonesha ana utapia mlo mkali. Ili kukusaidia mtoto wako kurudia katika hali yake bora ya lishe, nakupatia rufaa na nitarudia siku chache kufatilia kuhusu rufaa.

Ili kukusaidia mtoto wako apate matibabu, nitahakikisha nitampa rufaa ya wa tatizo la upungufu wa damu.

Mtoto wako ana dalili za hatari hizi zinahitaji kushungulikiwa kwa haraka. Mpeleke mtoto kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu. ikiwa kituo kimefungwa nenda hospitali iliyopo karibu nawe.

Eleza yafuatayo kuhusu umuhimu wa kumnyonyesha mtoto njiti angalau kwa masaa 2-3. -> Maziwa ya mama ni chakula bora sana kwa mtoto njiti kwani humkinga na maambukizi. ->Watoto njiti wanatakiwa mwanyonyeshwe mara kwa mara kwasababu huwa wananyonya kidogo tu ->Ikiwa mtoto atalala zaidi ya masaa 2 , muamshe kwa kugusagusa kwenye viganja au migunii ili umnyonyeshe hata nyakati za usiku.
ULIZA: Je mtoto wako ananyonya vizuri?.

_Fungua mjadala kwa kuanza kutoa maana ya "Mtoto njiti'':_ ->Mtoto njiti ni mtoto aliyezaliwa na uzito usiozitidi gram 2000 ambao ni uzito mdogo ambao si wa kawaida kwa watoto wanaozaliwa sasa. ->Uzito mdogo zaidi ni ule usiozidi gram 1500 wakati mtoto anapozalia
Ningependa kuzungumza na wewe kuhusu kunyonyesha ULIZA: Je ushawahi kunyonyesha?
Wapongeze wanafamilia ikiwa mtoto ananyonyeshwa maziwa ya mama tu

Wacha tujifunze jinsi ya kumueka mtoto vizuri mtoto wakati wa kumnyonyesha. Je unaweza kuniomnesha jinsi gani unamueka mtoto wako?. ->_Msaidie mama kuboresha jinsi ya kumueka mtoto: muombe amgusishe mtoto ncha ya ziwa kwenye _kwenye midomo yake , subiria hadi mtoto afungue mdomo wake vizuri, na muingizie mtoto ziwa karibu na mdomo wa chini. &nbso;_ Muombe mama akuoneshe jinsi anavyomueka mtoto wake wakati wa kumnyonyesha
Jadiliana na mama kwanini hamnyonyeshi mtoto wake mara kwa mara na kama inavyopaswa.

SEMA: Mtoto mtoto aliyezaliwa mdogo anaweza kuwa na mambo mengi ya kuzingatia kifya kuliko mtoto mwengine. Hivyo kuna umuhimu wa kumuangalia dalili za hatari, kumueka katika hali ya ujoto na kuwanyonyesha inavyopaswa ili kumfanya awe afya bora. Tutajifunza kuhusu mambo ambayo mtoto njiti yanaweza kumkuta na jinsi ya kumsaidia
Hakuna kimoja wapo kati ya hivyo.
Je mtoto alikunywa au kula chochote kati ya hivi vituatavyo jana mchana au usiku?
Ni muda gani mama alianza kumshonyesha mtoto wake punde baada ya kumzaa?
zaidi ya mara tano kwa siku
ULIZA: Ni kwa kiasi gani unamnyonyesha mtoto wako, Ikiwemo usiku na mchana?

Kamba ya kitovu cha mtoto wako kinakauka na hata kuanguka, kikawaida ndani ya wiki au wiki mbili baada ya kuzaliwa , kwa sasa jaribu kuitunza seheme vizuri:->_Weka kitovu kikavu, kiwache kipate upepo kusaidia kukauka. Ishushe pempas/kitambaa cha mtoto wako chini kwa mbele kuzuia kukifunika kitovu.->_ Usiweke kitu chochote kwenye kitvou_inaweza kuwa hatari kwa mtoto wako ikiwa utaingiza kitu chochote kwenye kitovu.->_Endelea kusafisha mtoto kwa kitambaa. Ikiwa hakuna madhara kitovu kikipata maji maji, kumkogesha mtoto kwa kitambaa kunaweza kusaidia kitovu kuwa kikavu. ->_Kiache kitovu kianguke chenyewe . Jizuie kutamani kukivuta kitovu.
Matunzo ya mtoto Njiti- Matunzo ya kitovu

Mshauri mama juu ya kudumisha usafi, ikiwemo kuosha mikoni mara kwa mara kwa sabuni na maji, mueleze kwamba kujikinga na maambukizi ni muhimu Zaidi kwa mtoto mtoto Njiti kuliko mtoto wa kawaida kwa sababu mtoto Njiti yupo kwenye hatari Zaidi ya maambukizi.

SEMA: Kangaroo Mother Care ni njia ya matunzo kwa mtoto Njiti kwa ajili ya kumfanya mtoto aendelee kuwa na joto stahiki. Njia hii huhusisha mtoto kuchukuliwa kwa kutumia makutano ya ngozi kwa ngozi._Sisitiza kwamba mtu yoyote anaweza kufanya KMC.E

Pitia hatua zifuatazo katika kumuweka mtoto kwa njia ya ngozi kwa ngozi na muangalie mama wakati anafanya KMC.->Mvue nguo mtoto isipokuwa nepi, kofia na soksi.->Muweke mtoto upright kati ya kifua cha mlezi pamoja na kifua cha mtoto -> Weka miguu ya mtoto sambamba na mbavu za mama na kilekeze kichwa cha mtoto upande mmoja.->Mfunike mtoto na nguo maalum itakayofungwa kumzunguka mama na mtoto.-> Sasa mama anaweza kuvaa shati, au sweta akitaka. ->Mwambie mama amnyonyeshe mtoto mara kwa mara kadri ya mtoto anavyotaka kunyonya lakini angalau masaa 2.->Ni muhimu saana mtoto kupata makutano ya ngozi kwa ngozi kwa muda wa masaa 20 kila siku.

ULIZA: Je unajua kwa nini watoto watoto njiti wanatakiwa kukaa sehem yenye joto la ziada kwa siku zao za mwanzo baada ya kuzaliwa?->Watoto wadogo wana mafuta kuliko watoto wakubwa, kwa hyo kuwa na joto lisilotosha linaweza kuwa ni tatizo. Hakikisha mtoto wako yuko mkavu na mwenye joto mda wote. ->Kangaroo mother care (KMC) ni njia nzuri Zaidi kwa mamam kumfanya mtoto awe na joto stahiki mda wote. Muingiliano wa ngozi kwa ngozi unasaidia joto la mtoto kuwa la kawaida.
Matunzo ya mtoto Njiti----matunzo ya ngozi kwa ngozi (KMC)

Wewe na mwenza wako au wana familia mna jukumu kwa ajili ya maendeleo ya akili na ubongo ya mtoto, na miaka na miezi hii ya mwanzo inapaswa kutumika kwa ajili ya ukuwaji.Mtoto aliyezaliwa anaweza kuona vitu, kuhisi vitu, na anaweza kupoke na kujibu sauti anazoskia.Mtoto Mtoto anaweza kutwambiw ikiwa ana njaa au hana furaha kwa kutumia sauti anayotoa. Mtoto anapenda kuona sura ya mama na baba yake au walezi wake wengine wa karibu.. [see translation in EFFECTS job aids] Kila tunapowapa nafasi watoto wetu kuielewa dunia (kwa kucheza, kujfiunza vitu vipya, kuona vitu vipya na kuendana na wanayofanya wengine) , ndivyo taarifa huingia Zaidi kwenye ubongo wao.For your newborn baby, you cankwa ajili ya mtoto wako aliyezaliwa: [Siku 0-7:] ->Zungumza na Mwimbie mtoto[Days 8 to month 6]: [use points in image in dropbox: Dropbox (D-tree)\DTree\Country Programs\Tanzania\Projects\Botnar Foundation ZNZ\Technical\Design\App Content\Protocols for Digitization\2019.03.12 Infant-Child\Shortened versions\5. Essential newborn care - shortened.docx]

Ningependa tujadili jinsi ya kukihifadhi vinyesi vya Watoto , Kinyesi cha mtoto kinaweza kuwa hatari au chanjo cha maradhi ULIZA ni wapi mtoto wako anajisaidia? ni wapi unapokitupa kinyesi cha mtoto wako?
ULIZA Je una choo nyumbani mwako?
Choo cha shimo kisicho na paipu
Choo chenye kikalio ambacho kina paipu
Kutumia choo inaweza kusadia kukuepusha na usambaaji wa maradhi. Ikiwa upo uwezekano, ni vyema ukawa na choo ndani mwako.

Mueleze mama hatari ya kinyesi cha mtoto na njia salama ya kukihifadhi: Njia salama ya kukihifadhi kinyesi cha mtoto ni kumsaidia mtoto aweze kujisaidia chooni au kukitupa kinyesi cha watoto wachanga kwenye shimo la choo Upo ushahidi unaoonesha kuwa kinyesi cha mtoto ni hatari zaidi kuliko cha mtu mzima. Vinyesi vya watoto ni vyema vikahifadhiwa kama vya watu wazima, kutumia njia salama ya kuvitupa ambayo itahakikisha watu hawakutani navyo au havileti uchafuzi kwenye kaya.

Ni muhimu kunawa mikono yako kwa maji na sabuni kabla kushika mtoto: Watoto wachanga wanaweza kupata maambukizi kwa urahisi zaidi kuliko mtu mzima au Maambukizo yanayoweza kuhama kutoka kwa mtu mzima kwenda kwa mtoto yanaweza kuwa hatari kwa sababu kinga ya watoto bado ni dhaifu / haijatengenezwa kikamilifu hivyo unawaji wa mikono kwa usahihi ni mojawapo ya njia bora sana za kuzuia maambukizi
Mambo yanayopaswa kuwepo ili kukosha mikono vizuri -> Tunahitaji maji safi yenye kutiririka na sabuni -> Ikiwa maji na sabuni hamna unaweza kutumia jivu

Nyakati muhimu za kukosha mikono ni: -> Baada ya kutawaza -> Kabla ya kumlisha mtoto -> kabla ya unapotaka kula -> Baada ya kumsafisha mtoto -> Kabla ya kuandaa chakula -> Baada ya kutengeneza nyama, samaki au poultry
